description_long: This object consists of a group of fourteen yellow diamond-shaped traffic signs, each mounted on a slender metallic post. The signs display a variety of standard road warnings and instructions, including symbols for merging lanes, dead ends, pedestrian crossings, and traffic lights. The signs are arranged in a slightly curved line, with each sign facing outward and clearly visible from the front. The posts appear to be made of metal, likely galvanized steel, and the signs themselves are likely made of reflective metal or aluminum with painted or printed graphics. The rear of each sign is plain, showing the unmarked yellow backing and the mounting brackets. This collection is typical of what might be found in a municipal storage yard, training facility, or as a display for educational or planning purposes.
description: A set of fourteen yellow diamond-shaped traffic signs with various road symbols and text, each mounted on a metal post and arranged in a curved line.
description_view_0: The signs are viewed edge-on, showing only the thin profiles of the posts and sign panels.
description_view_1: The front faces of all signs are visible, displaying a variety of road symbols and text in black on yellow backgrounds.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the signs are seen edge-on from the opposite side, with only the thin edges and posts visible.
description_view_3: The backs of the signs are visible, showing plain yellow surfaces and the mounting hardware on the posts.
attribute_sign_text: MERGE LEFT, RIGHT LANE ENDS, LEFT LANE ENDS, DEAD END
attribute_symbol_or_pictogram: Arrows indicating merging and lane ends, pedestrian crossing, traffic light, bicycle, intersection, and other standard road symbols.
attribute_location_context: urban roadside, intersections, or roadwork zones
